history.content:
<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right of my location. However, the cells directly in front of me and diagonally left are lava (L), which are deadly and must be avoided. There is an empty floor cell directly to my right, which I cannot reach immediately because I am facing north. I will turn right to face that direction and access the safe path leading towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I am facing east after turning right. The goal G is visible to the left behind walls, so I cannot go directly that way. The cell in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I should turn left to face north (back to the original direction) to explore alternative paths.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The observation looks like it returned to the previous one where I am facing north again with lava cells in front and to the left. The path to the right looks clear if I turn right and move forward. I will turn right first to face east and then can move forward safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I have turned right and now face east again. The cell in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is not an option. The path left and right is blocked or contains lava. I will turn left to face north to explore other routes.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The observation returned to the initial view with the goal visible to the right and lava cells ahead and left. The forward cell is lava, so I cannot move forward. I had turned right in the previous step but the map reset to the same view. To reach the goal, I will try turning left to find a different path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north with a wall directly ahead and lava to my right. Moving forward is not possible because of the wall. Turning right or left could help explore another direction. I will turn left to try moving west.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing west with a wall to my right, a wall ahead, and unwalkable cells at the bottom. The path forward is blocked. I should turn left to face south and check if the path is open in that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing south with lava cells directly ahead and to the left, and a goal visible two cells left and two cells forward. Moving forward or left is dangerous due to lava. I should turn right to face west towards the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing west with walls ahead and to the sides. The path forward is blocked. I will turn left to face south, hoping for a possible exit route in that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing south with lava cells directly ahead and to the left, so moving forward is unsafe. The goal is to my left. I will turn right to face west and try to find a safe path around the lava to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
This observation looks similar to the first one, indicating I am now facing west again with walls directly ahead and around. Since I previously turned left to face south, now I am facing west without a clear path forward. I should turn left again to face south and explore that direction for a way around the walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am again facing south with lava cells directly ahead. To avoid lava, I should not move forward. The goal is to the left, so I will turn left to face east to find a safe path towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal G two cells to my right, but there is lava (L) in front and to the right, so moving forward is deadly. I will turn right to face west, aiming to find a safer path around the lava towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing south and there is lava directly ahead, which is dangerous. The goal is visible to my left. To avoid the lava and move towards the goal, I will turn left to face east.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible two cells to my right. The path directly in front of me is blocked by lava, so moving forward is unsafe. I will turn right to face east to try to navigate towards the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
